sourceSummary.oneLine: User manual and service information for the HnX-PB portable X-ray system, including intended use, safety warnings, regulatory standards, EMC compliance, operation, maintenance, calibration, and product information.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Provides instructions, precautions, regulatory information, operation procedures, and service information for use and maintenance of the HnX-PB portable X-ray system.
keyPoints: The HnX-PB is a battery-powered portable X-ray system designed and manufactured by H&abyz.
The portable X-ray system may be used for diagnostic imaging of body extremities and is not for mammography use.
The device is not intended to replace a stationary radiographic system.
Portable diagnostic X-ray equipment shall only be used when it is medically inadvisable to move a patient to a fixed radiographic or fixed fluoroscopic installation.
The HnX-PB conforms to IEC 60601-1-2:2020 for immunity and emissions.
The product information section identifies the product name as Portable X-ray System and the model name as HnX-PB.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: Manufacturer: H&abyz Co., Ltd. 2-dong, 41-16 Cheoinseong-ro, Namsa-eup, Cheoin-gu, Yongin-si, Gyeonggi-do, Republic of Korea

entities.name: HnX-PB
entities.evidence: This portable radiographic system (Model: HnX-PB) consists of an LCD display with up and down soft-keys for controlling kV, an X-ray generator (line-powered transformer), an X-ray tube assembly, a collimator and a cart.

entities.name: Food and Drug Administration
entities.evidence: Content of Premarket Submission for Management of Cybersecurity in Medical Devices Guidance for Industry and Food and Drug Administration Staff Document Issued on: October 2, 2014

entities.name: Federal Communications Commission
entities.evidence: FCC SUB PART C This section of the FCC regulations covers the rules and requirements for devices that intentionally generate and emit radio frequency energy, including low-power communication devices and systems

entities.name: H&abyz A1417MCW / A1717MCW / F1417MCW
entities.evidence: H&abyz A1417MCW / A1717MCW / F1417MCW declared in K223930 Or alternatives U.S. 510(k) cleared.

concepts.name: Intended use for diagnostic imaging of body extremities
concepts.evidence: The portable x-ray system may be used for diagnostic imaging of body extremities.

concepts.name: Portable diagnostic X-ray use restriction
concepts.evidence: This equipment shall only be used when it is medically inadvisable to move a patient to a fixed radiographic or fixed fluoroscopic installation.

concepts.name: Electrical safety classification
concepts.evidence: Type of protection against electrical shock Class I or Internally powered ME equipment

concepts.name: Ingress protection rating IPX0
concepts.evidence: Degree of protection against ingress of water IPX0

concepts.name: EMC compliance
concepts.evidence: HnX-PB conforms to this IEC60601-1-2:2020 standard for both immunity and emissions.

concepts.name: Cybersecurity risk management
concepts.evidence: The cybersecurity risk of the HnX-PB have been addressed to assure that no new or increased cybersecurity risks were introduced as a part of device risk analysis.

concepts.name: Authorized operator controls
concepts.evidence: This device requires facility-implemented access controls. Failure to implement proper procedural safeguards violates radiation safety regulations

concepts.name: Annual inspection and calibration
concepts.evidence: The following inspection and maintenance for the unit shall be performed once every year, and handled by a service person only:

evidence.claim: The document is a user manual for the HnX-PB portable X-ray system.
evidence.evidenceText: User Manual (HnX-PB) Portable X-ray System

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is a portable X-ray system.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB is a portable X-ray System.

evidence.claim: The manual only pertains to the HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: This User Manual only pertains to the HnX-PB and does not serve for any other products of the company.

evidence.claim: H&abyz Co., Ltd. is identified as the manufacturer.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer: H&abyz Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: The product name is Portable X-ray System and the model name is HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Portable X-ray System Model name: HnX-PB

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is used with a film-cassette or flat-panel detector.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB is used with a film-cassette or flat-panel detector.

evidence.claim: The device is battery-powered and designed and manufactured by H&abyz.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is a battery-powered portable X-ray System, designed and manufactured by H&abyz.

evidence.claim: The system is for X-ray imaging and diagnosis in facilities with portable or fixing sites.
evidence.evidenceText: The system is for X-ray imaging and diagnosis in facilities with portable or fixing sites.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is not intended for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: This HnX-PB is not intended for mammography.

evidence.claim: The portable X-ray system may be used for diagnostic imaging of body extremities.
evidence.evidenceText: The portable x-ray system may be used for diagnostic imaging of body extremities.

evidence.claim: The device is not intended to replace a stationary radiographic system.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not intended to replace a stationary radiographic system, which may be required for full optimization of image quality and radiation exposure for different exam types.

evidence.claim: The equipment shall only be used when it is medically inadvisable to move a patient to a fixed radiographic or fixed fluoroscopic installation.
evidence.evidenceText: This equipment shall only be used when it is medically inadvisable to move a patient to a fixed radiographic or fixed fluoroscopic installation.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is designed for radiographic uses only and is not intended for fluoroscopic use.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB is designed for radiographic uses only and not intended for fluoroscopic use or other associated applications.

evidence.claim: The device does not apply to mammography and dental radiography.
evidence.evidenceText: The device does not apply to mammography and dental radiography.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is specified as Class I and IPX0 equipment under EN 60601-1 standards.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB is specified as Class I and IPX0 equipment under EN 60601-1 standards.

evidence.claim: The type of protection against electrical shock is Class I or internally powered ME equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: Type of protection against electrical shock Class I or Internally powered ME equipment

evidence.claim: The degree of protection against ingress of water is IPX0.
evidence.evidenceText: Degree of protection against ingress of water IPX0

evidence.claim: The mode of operation is continuous operation.
evidence.evidenceText: Mode of operation Continuous operation

evidence.claim: The device is not suitable for use in the presence of a flammable anesthetic mixture or oxygen rich environment.
evidence.evidenceText: Not suitable for use in the presence of a flammable anesthetic mixture with air or with oxygen or nitrous oxide Not suitable for use in the oxygen rich environment

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB conforms to IEC 60601-1-2:2020 for both immunity and emissions.
evidence.evidenceText: HnX-PB conforms to this IEC60601-1-2:2020 standard for both immunity and emissions.

evidence.claim: Applicable FDA and U.S. regulatory references include 21 CFR 1020 Subchapter J and 21 CFR 1020.30.
evidence.evidenceText: The device also conforms to the following: • 21 CFR 1020 Subchapter J: Performance Standards for Ionizing Radiation Emitting Products • 21 CFR 1020.30: Diagnostic x-ray system and their major components

evidence.claim: FCC Sub Part C is listed as a regulatory standard for intentional RF energy emitting devices.
evidence.evidenceText: FCC SUB PART C This section of the FCC regulations covers the rules and requirements for devices that intentionally generate and emit radio frequency energy, including low-power communication devices and systems

evidence.claim: The maximum output of the X-ray generator is 720 W.
evidence.evidenceText: The maximum output of X-ray generator is 720W

evidence.claim: The X-ray tube output voltage range is 50 to 90 kV.
evidence.evidenceText: The X-ray Tube output voltage of generator is 50~90kV

evidence.claim: The software or firmware is HnX_P1 version 00.12.
evidence.evidenceText: Software(Firmware): HnX_P1 Version: 00.12

evidence.claim: The estimated product lifetime is up to 7 years under appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.
evidence.evidenceText: The estimated product lifetime is up to 7 years under appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.

evidence.claim: Regular inspection should be performed at least once a year.
evidence.evidenceText: In order to ensure the safety of patients and operator, to maintain the performance and reliability of the device, be sure to perform regular inspection at least once a year.

evidence.claim: Annual preventive maintenance includes checking oil tank spills or level drop, annual calibration, and checking collimator light field deviation.
evidence.evidenceText: The following inspection and maintenance for the unit shall be performed once every year, and handled by a service person only: a) Check if the oil tank of the Portable X-ray System has any oil spill from the outer wall of the tank, any liquid surface drop in the tank. b) Calibrate the Portable X-ray System annually. c) Check if the collimator light field deviates from the tube radiation field.
